message:
perf(providers): replace heap-allocated String fields with &'static str
and add coverage-guided fuzz modules to all provider stream parsers

Type-safety & allocation elimination (all 8 providers):
- Convert `role: String` → `role: &'static str` across all provider
  response/delta structs, eliminating per-token heap allocations on the
  hot streaming path
- Convert `r#type: String` → `r#type: &'static str` for content block
  type discriminators (anthropic, azure, openai, openai_responses, etc.)
- Add `to_static_role()` helper in azure and openai providers that maps
  known role strings ("assistant", "user", "system", "tool") to static
  references with a fallback leak for unknown roles
- Anthropic provider: major refactoring of flat structs into tagged serde
  enums — AnthropicContentBlock (text|thinking|tool_use|image|document),
  AnthropicDelta (text_delta|thinking_delta|input_json_delta), and
  AnthropicStopReason enum — replacing stringly-typed match arms with
  exhaustive pattern matching for compile-time safety
- Anthropic handle_content_block_start now returns StreamEvent directly
  instead of Option<StreamEvent>, simplifying call sites

Fuzz infrastructure (FUZZ-P2.4):
- Add `#[cfg(feature = "fuzzing")] pub mod fuzz` to anthropic, azure,
  cohere, gemini, openai, openai_responses, and vertex providers
- Each fuzz module exposes a `Processor` struct wrapping the provider's
  `process_stream_event` with arbitrary byte → serde_json::Value →
  StreamEvent pipeline for coverage-guided fuzzing of SSE parsing logic
- Bedrock gets type-safety changes only (no fuzz module, as it delegates
  to anthropic internally)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
